strongest_failure_path: We sized Japan Exchange Group at ¥2,190 as a durable croupier monopoly, trusting normalized owner cash of ¥70 per share and a four-component contract base of ¥1,800 per share despite cycle classification at peak and contract base return of -2.76% per year (total synthesis 3.19%) far below the ~15% accumulate hurdle. FY2025 was treated as partially normalized when cash equities ADV had already surged +31.9% YoY to ¥7.52T and clearing collateral income inflated the revenue bridge. The post-boom mean reversion was sharper than the downside_reserve component assumed: turnover fell toward pre-FY2025 mid-cycle levels (five-year revenue ¥133–162B), clearing pass-through income normalized with lower collateral balances and rates, and spot EPS dropped from ¥76.81 toward the contract falsifier band below ¥55 per share. The market re-rated the equity from roughly 26× peak earnings toward mid-teens on trough owner cash, so we suffered both an earnings miss and multiple compression. Hold-period total return landed near the bear envelope (-6.3% per year) rather than the 1.7% Lawrence base or 3.19% synthesis case—severe underperformance on a franchise-quality position held for toll-booth durability at the wrong point in the volume cycle.
